title: Guettardella microphylla
intro: Guettardella microphylla, also known as the small-leaved Guettardella, is a type of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wound healing, fever reduction, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Guettardella microphylla include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Guettardella microphylla is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications for skin and wound care.
